summary: Issue #84623 remains the canonical open tracker for the non-streaming dispatchReplyFromConfig duplicate-final-payload bug. Current main still iterates every replyResult entry through sendFinalPayload with no per-turn final duplicate guard. Prior PR #94294 is closed and carries a blocked merge-risk: message-delivery label, so it is retained only as non-mutating related context while the safe executable path is a new narrow fix from the canonical issue and current-main proof.

actions.reason: Real bug remains on current main and no viable open canonical PR owns the exact fix.
Same message-delivery symptom family, different channel/failover root cause and separate maintainer follow-up.
Different root cause and repair surface; should not be closed or merged in this cluster.
Blocked-label lineage cannot be used for executable repair planning.
Build a new narrow fix PR from canonical issue #84623 and current-main proof; do not use blocked PR #94294 as source lineage.

repair_candidate.summary: Add a narrow per-turn final-dispatch dedupe guard in dispatchReplyFromConfig so byte-identical non-streaming final payload entries for one turn are delivered once, while preserving intentionally distinct same-content finals that differ by delivery-visible metadata or route identity.
repair_candidate.pr_title: fix(reply): dedupe duplicate non-streaming final replies
repair_candidate.pr_body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #84623, where non-streaming dispatchReplyFromConfig can deliver identical final reply payload entries twice for one turn.

## Why This Change Was Made

Current main still normalizes replyResult to an array and sends each non-suppressed entry through sendFinalPayload without a per-turn duplicate-final guard. The fix belongs at the shared dispatcher choke point so channels using non-streaming final replies do not receive duplicate identical text or media payloads for one inbound message.

## User Impact

Channels using non-streaming final replies, including media directive delivery, should no longer send duplicate identical text or media replies for one inbound message. Intentionally distinct finals must remain deliverable when their delivery-visible identity differs.

## Evidence

- Current main 17777b3a9ff18e0262e55815d46c168933c82b8e still loops over every replyResult final entry in src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts.
- Add regression coverage for duplicate identical final entries and for preserved distinct same-content entries.
- Run pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.reply-dispatch.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-payloads.test.ts.
- Run pnpm check:changed.
credit_notes: Base the implementation on canonical issue #84623 and current-main source proof, not on blocked PR lineage.
Keep the patch narrow and preserve any reporter attribution required by the PR template.
